{{Quote|Every day is a good day to make a credit.|Woetar|<ref name="FT" />}}
'''Woetar''' was a [[Zarian]] con man.<ref name="FT" /> Sometime between [[5 ABY]] and [[9 ABY]],<ref name="FT Date">''[[The Politics of Contraband]]'' dates itself to before ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', but still within the [[New Republic era]]. {{Blog|holocron|24|Star Wars Eras}} dates the beginning of the New Republic era to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. The ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' to [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. Thus, ''The Politics of Contraban'' takes place sometime between 5 ABY and 9 ABY.</ref> he won 1,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] against a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tekurr'k]].<ref name="FT" /> Sometime between 5 ABY and 9 ABY,<ref name="FT Date" /> he agreed to help a [[Courier/Legends|courier]] deliver a passage to a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Karren]] on the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] planet of [[Antiquity]]. As the ship was too big to land on the planet, it was forced to stay in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. At this time, Woetar was stationed in the city of [[Lis'an]]. However, in preparing to run the job, Woetar ran afoul of a displeased former customer who surrounded his ship with goons, preventing him from escaping. Woetar noticed the arrival of the ship belonging to the spacer he'd met on Tekurr'k; out of options, he approached the spacer to make a deal that would enable him to complete his [[Mission to Antiquity|courier run]] while eluding his enemies. The spacer agreed to hear Woetar out.

Woetar wore a loose fitting tunic and had a variety of technology to his use, including a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and a [[Sporting blaster/Legends|sporting blaster]]. Woetar had thick, resilient [[Color/Legends|green]] skin and a high, nasal voice. He used his humorous, non-threatening appearance to his advantage in various confidence schemes. He was streetwise and had knowledge of [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]], astrography, and [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]].<ref name="FT" />

==Behind the scenes==
Woetar appeared in "[[Free Time]]," an adventure in ''[[The Politics of Contraband]]'', for the [[West End Games]] ''[[Star Wars]]'' roleplaying game. It was written by [[Gary Haynes]] and published in [[September]] [[1992]].<ref name="FT" />
